author: yuxu42
title: smoke_ConvertCPULayerTest - Value of: primTypeCheck(primType) is unexpected
signature: primType is unexpected unknown Expected ref|Flaky Test

failed_jobs.name: CPU functional tests / CPU functional tests
failed_jobs.symptom: smoke_ConvertCPULayerTest_4D_Dynamic assertion: primType is unexpected unknown_i8, Expected ref_i8 at cpu_test_utils.cpp:248

key_errors: primType is unexpected : unknown_i8 Expected : ref_i8
root_cause: The ARM64/ACL ConvertCPULayerTest is intermittently failing when the ACL backend selects 'unknown_i8' as the primitive type instead of the expected 'ref_i8' for an i8->u8 conversion with nchw format. This test failure is unrelated to the PR under review (PR #36890 fixes Paddle frontend expand_v2 dynamic-rank Broadcast); the same ConvertCPULayerTest class with different precisions (f32, i8) has been observed failing on unrelated PRs in the last 24 hours, indicating a pre-existing flaky behavior in the ARM64 ACL primitive type selection.
reproduction_steps: Run: ov_cpu_func_tests --gtest_filter=*smoke_ConvertCPULayerTest_4D_Dynamic*inputPRC=i8*targetPRC=u8*primitive=acl* on ARM64 hardware with ACL backend
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed jobs — the failure is unrelated to the PR change and has been observed as intermittent across multiple PRs
Investigate and fix the ACL backend primitive type selection for i8/u8 conversion with nchw layout on ARM64 in src/plugins/intel_cpu/
Consider adding the test to an expected-failures list while the ACL issue is being diagnosed
prevention_strategies: Improve primitive-type fallback handling in the ACL backend so that missing i8 support falls back to ref cleanly and does not produce 'unknown' type, or disable the ACL path for i8->u8 nchw conversions until properly supported.
historical_context: Same test class (smoke_ConvertCPULayerTest_4D_Dynamic with primitive=acl) failed with f32 precision on run 29552016907 (PR #36929) approximately 11 minutes earlier. Earlier instance with i32 precision was recorded on 2026-07-08 (run 28957097935, pattern cb7d14bccc236de4). Multiple PRs are being blocked by this pre-existing ACL issue.
